steps.title: Use the cylinder volume formula
steps.content: A cylinder's volume is $V=\pi r^2h$. The diameter is $26$ cm, so the radius is $13$ cm, and the height is $5$ cm.

steps.title: Identify the radius and height
steps.content: From a diameter of 26 cm, the radius is $r = 26 \div 2 = 13$ cm. The height is $h = 5$ cm.

steps.title: Apply the cylinder volume formula
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 13$ and $h = 5$ into the cylinder volume formula:
$$V = \pi (13)^2 (5)$$
$$V = \pi \cdot 169 \cdot 5$$
$$V = 845\pi$$
That is choice C, the correct answer.
